name: Personal Data Protection Law (Law No. 27 of 2022)
summary: Indonesia's first comprehensive personal data protection law, enacted October 2022 with a two-year transition period making it fully enforceable from 17 October 2024. It covers both electronic and non-electronic personal data processing and applies to entities within and outside Indonesia processing data of Indonesian residents.
keyRequirements: Obtain valid and explicit consent for personal data processing
Mandatory breach notification to data subjects within 72 hours of discovering a breach
Data subject rights including access, correction, deletion, portability, and withdrawal of consent
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing
Appoint a Data Protection Officer for large-scale processing or sensitive data
Cross-border data transfer restrictions requiring equivalent protection in receiving country
Maintain records of data processing activities
penalties: Administrative fines up to 2% of annual revenue. Criminal penalties: imprisonment up to 4-6 years and/or fines up to IDR 4-6 billion (~USD 250,000-400,000). For corporations, monetary penalties can be up to 10x the individual maximum. Enforced by the Ministry of Communications and Digital (interim, pending establishment of dedicated DPA).
